traditionalFoods.name: Brački Vitalac
traditionalFoods.description: One of the most authentic, ancient culinary traditions on Brač, protected as a national cultural heritage. Vitalac consists of lamb or kid offal (heart, lungs, liver) skewered, wrapped in lamb's caul, and then wrapped tightly in intestines before being roasted over open embers. It is crispy on the outside, tender inside, and traditionally served sliced as an appetizer while waiting for the main spit-roast lamb.

traditionalFoods.name: Hrapoćuša Cake
traditionalFoods.description: Hailing from the stone village of Dol, this sweet, heavy cake is named after the local craggy, reddish breccia stone ('hrapoćuša') because its rough topping of walnuts and baked sugar mimics the cave formations. It is sweet, extremely calorie-dense (perfect for cyclists!), and protected by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage asset.

traditionalFoods.name: Pročić
traditionalFoods.description: An incredibly old, traditional shepherd snack. It is made by melting young, unsalted Brač cheese in caramelized sugar. It must be eaten immediately while warm and gooey, combining savory, rich cheese notes with deep sweet caramel.

traditionalFoods.name: Spit-Roast Lamb & Peka
traditionalFoods.description: Due to the island's aromatic wild herbs (like sage, rosemary, and thyme), Brač lamb has a completely unique flavor profile. Whether slow-roasted on a spit or baked under a heavy iron bell ('peka') surrounded by potatoes and wild herbs, this is the definitive culinary centerpiece of any trip to the island.
